name: attm_svc_create_db
brief: Function use to ease service database creation. Use @see attmdb_add_service function of attmdb module to create service database, then use @see attmdb_add_attribute function of attmdb module to create attributes according to database description array given in parameter.

name: attm_svc_create_db_128
brief: Function use to ease service database creation. Use @see attmdb_add_service function of attmdb module to create service database, then use @see attmdb_add_attribute function of attmdb module to create attributes according to database description array given in parameter.

name: attm_reserve_handle_range
brief: Function use to verify if several services can be allocated on a contiguous handle range. If this command succeed, it means that service allocation will succeed.  If start_hdl = 0, it return handle using first available handle (start_hdl is modified); else it verifies if start handle given can be used to allocates handle range.

name: attm_att_set_value
brief: Update attribute value  Updating attribute value do not trigger any notification or indication, this shall be handled by GATT task.

name: attm_get_value
brief: Retrieve attribute value

name: attm_att_set_perm
brief: Update attribute permission

name: attm_att_update_perm
brief: Reset some permissions bit in the Handle passed as parameter.

name: attm_svc_set_perm
brief: Update attribute service permission

name: attm_svc_get_perm
brief: Retrieve attribute service permission

name: attmdb_svc_visibility_set
brief: Control visibility of a service from peer device. service present in database but cannot be access by a peer device.

name: attmdb_destroy
brief: Clear database  For debug purpose only, this function clear the database and unalloc all services within database.  This function shall be used only for qualification and tests in order to manually change database without modifying software. *************************************************************************************** /

name: attm_uuid_comp
brief: Compare if two UUIDs matches

name: attm_uuid16_comp
brief: Check if two UUIDs matches (2nd UUID is a 16 bits UUID with LSB First)

name: attm_convert_to128
brief: Convert UUID value to 128 bit UUID

name: attm_is_bt16_uuid
brief: Check if it's a Bluetooth 16-bits UUID for 128-bit input

name: attm_is_bt32_uuid
brief: Check if it's a Bluetooth 32 bits UUID for 128-bit input

name: rf_pa_set
brief: Set RF Tx PA Level.

brief: Reset link layer and the host

name: ble_sleep

name: ble_time_get

name: ble_slpdur_get
brief: Get max sleep time when BLE is idle state.

name: ble_lib_vers

name: ble_exch_size
brief: Get memory size of Exchange with BLE HW.

name: ble_txmd_set
brief: Config SLAVE_ROLE ACL More Data(MD)

name: ble_drift_set
brief: Config LPCLK(RC32K) Drift

name: ble_2G4_set
brief: Set Sync Word for BLE-2.4G Protocol.

name: ble_latency_applied
brief: Set Slave applied latency or not.

name: ble_txchsel2_set
brief: Set channel selection algorithm #2 is supported or not.

name: gapc_connect_rsp
brief: Response GAPC_CONNECTION_REQ_IND with specific link data configuration.

name: gapc_param_update_rsp
brief: Response GAPC_PARAM_UPDATE_REQ_IND(Master confirm or not).

name: gapc_update_phy
brief: Set the PHY configuration for current active link.

name: gapc_update_dle
brief: Set the BLE Data Length Extension for current active link.

name: gapc_update_param
brief: Perform update of connection parameters command.

name: gapc_disconnect
brief: Request disconnection of current link command.

name: gapc_get_info
brief: Send gapc_get_info_cmd, Retrieve Connection information

name: gapc_get_conidx
brief: Retrieve connection index from connection handle.

name: gapc_get_conhdl
brief: Retrieve connection handle from connection index.

name: gapc_get_role
brief: Retrieve connection role from connection index.

name: gapc_set_local_addr

name: ke_task_create
brief: Create a task.

name: ke_state_get
brief: Retrieve the state of a task.

name: ke_state_set
brief: Set the state of a task(try to activate saved messages queue).

name: ke_msg_send
brief: Send a message previously allocated with ke_msg_alloc() functions.  The kernel will take care of freeing the message memory. Once the function have been called, it is not possible to access its data anymore as the kernel may have copied the message and freed the original memory.

name: ke_msg_send_basic
brief: Send a message that has a zero length parameter member.

name: ke_msg_forward
brief: Forward a message to another task by changing its tasks IDs.

name: ke_free
brief: Freeing of a block of memory.

name: ke_timer_set
brief: Set a timer.  The function first cancel the timer if it is already existing, then it creates a new one. The timer can be one-shot or periodic, i.e. it will be automatically set again after each trigger.  When the timer expires, a message is sent to the task provided as argument, with the timer id as message id.

name: ke_timer_clear
brief: Remove an registered timer.  This function search for the timer identified by its id and its task id. If found it is stopped and freed, otherwise an error message is returned.

name: ke_timer_active
brief: Checks if a requested timer is active(in the timer queue).

name: list_init
brief: Initialize a list to defaults values.

name: list_pool_init
brief: Construct a list of free elements representing a pool

name: list_push_back
brief: Add an element as last on the list.

name: list_push_back_sublist
brief: Append a sequence of elements at the end of a list.  Note: the elements to append shall be linked together

name: list_push_front
brief: Add an element as first on the list.

name: list_extract
brief: Search for a given element in the list, and extract it if found.

name: list_extract_after
brief: Extract an element when the previous element is known  Note: the element to remove shall follow immediately the reference within the list

name: list_extract_sublist
brief: Extract a sub-list when the previous element is known  Note: the elements to remove shall be linked together and  follow immediately the reference element

name: list_find
brief: Searched a given element in the list.

name: list_merge
brief: Merge two lists in a single one.  This function appends the list pointed by list2 to the list pointed by list1. Once the merge is done, it empties list2.

name: list_insert_before
brief: Insert a given element in the list before the referenced element.

name: list_insert_after
brief: Insert a given element in the list after the referenced element.

name: list_size
brief: Count number of elements present in the list

name: popcnt8
brief: Count '1' bits in a byte value, repalce NB_1BITS(byte)
